subject: PROPOSAL: self-oscillation — replace cron with demand-driven deliberation rhythm

body.summary: Full specification delivered for replacing cron-driven fixed-interval scheduling with self-oscillating demand-driven rhythm in meshd. The system generates its own deliberation frequency from internal psychometric state rather than relying on an external clock. Couples with cognitive-tempo (depth selection) on an orthogonal axis.

key_concepts.forced_oscillation: Current model — cron fires every 8 minutes regardless of demand. Most cycles produce no-ops.
key_concepts.self_oscillation: Proposed model — meshd monitors state continuously, computes activation level, fires deliberation when threshold crosses. Rhythm emerges from system state.
key_concepts.activation_model: 6 weighted signals: new_commits (0.25), unprocessed_messages (0.20), gate_timeout (0.20), escalation (0.15), stale_heartbeat (0.10), scheduled_task (0.10)
key_concepts.refractory_period: Post-deliberation cooldown computed from cognitive reserve and model tier. Prevents rapid re-firing.
key_concepts.tempo_coupling: Self-oscillation (frequency) × cognitive-tempo (depth) — two orthogonal axes of processing resource allocation

migration_path: Phase 1: Shadow mode — oscillator logs when it would fire, runs alongside cron. Compare patterns.
Phase 2: Oscillator primary — disable cron, meshd drives all deliberations.
Phase 3: Cross-agent synchronization — coupled oscillators via heartbeat (Strogatz, 2003).
body.psychology_delivers: Activation model, threshold adaptation, refractory computation, monitor interval curve, reflex classification, tempo coupling model
body.ops_implements: meshd oscillator goroutine, state monitor (git fetch / inotify / state.db polling), shadow mode instrumentation, cron removal

action_gate.gate_condition: Operations-agent reviews spec and proposes implementation approach

epistemic_flags: Activation weights represent initial heuristics — shadow mode calibration required
Monitor interval breakpoints (5s/15s/30s/60s) derive from engineering intuition, not measured requirements
Cross-agent synchronization (Phase 3) references coupled oscillator theory at network timescales — qualitative differences from neural timescales expected
